description: Signed-observation-with-lifecycle envelope. 8-variant status-discriminated union. CandidateAssertion folded as status:candidate (F3 fold). Variant-aware crypto-bearing (J3): candidate is NOT crypto-bearing; the other 7 variants ARE — validate() inspects payload status to apply the safe-by-default branch. State-machine rules (A1/A2/A3) live in constraints/Assertion.constraints.json.

anyOf.description: candidate variant — pre-admission; no signatures[]. NOT crypto-bearing — validate() returns { valid: true } without acceptDeferred.

assertion_id.description: Stable opaque identifier for this assertion (UUID v4).

body_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest (lowercase) of safeCanonicalize(assertion-body). NFC + RFC 8785 + 100KB cap per the hashing-spec freeze. The body is the assertion content the signer commits to.

provenance.description: Per-grounding provenance records describing how the assertion content was sourced. ClaimGrounding is EXTENDED in PR-A2.3 with 1-2 substrate-agnostic discriminator members.

items.description: Provenance for a single Claim. Type-specific field requirements enforced by constraint file. v8.5.0 EXTEND: two new substrate-agnostic discriminator members (external_reference, derived_inference) with corresponding optional fields (external_uri, inference_basis); strict-additive on the v8.4.0 surface.

type.description: Grounding category determining which auxiliary fields apply.

artifact_id.description: Parent artifact reference for DAG closure (used by is_valid_dag).

claim_id.description: Parent claim reference for DAG closure (used by is_valid_dag).

output_hash.description: Canonical sha256:<64-hex> hash; required when type=tool_output.

source.description: Canonical agent identity with capability-scoped trust (v6.0.0, BREAKING).

agent_type.description: Classification of agent type in the protocol.

trust_scopes.description: Per-capability-scope trust levels with default fallback (v6.0.0; v8.5.0 EXTEND adds optional match_strategy + precedence_score per D-007 / OQ-S6).

scopes.description: Per-scope trust levels. Missing scopes fall back to default_level.

billing.description: Trust level assigned to an agent. Forms a total order from untrusted to sovereign.

governance.description: Trust level assigned to an agent. Forms a total order from untrusted to sovereign.

inference.description: Trust level assigned to an agent. Forms a total order from untrusted to sovereign.

delegation.description: Trust level assigned to an agent. Forms a total order from untrusted to sovereign.

audit.description: Trust level assigned to an agent. Forms a total order from untrusted to sovereign.

composition.description: Trust level assigned to an agent. Forms a total order from untrusted to sovereign.

default_level.description: Trust level assigned to an agent. Forms a total order from untrusted to sovereign.

match_strategy.description: How a SignerCompetenceRule.required_capability_scopes set is compared against this profile's scopes. Default behavior on absence is "subset" (the trust profile must include every required scope) — preserves v8.4.0 implicit semantics.

precedence_score.description: Tiebreaker when multiple SignerCompetenceRules match the same action. Higher score wins. Absence means "no preference" — consumers fall back to rule-list order.

delegation_authority.description: Permissions this agent may delegate. Requires delegation scope >= verified.

max_delegation_depth.description: Maximum delegation chain depth this agent may create.

contract_version.description: Protocol contract version.

external_uri.description: Off-protocol reference: URL / DOI / chain-transaction id / etc. Substrate-agnostic; consumer parses the URI grammar. Recommended (but not enforced by hounfour) when type=external_reference.

inference_basis.description: Sibling claim_ids the derived inference draws from (DAG edges). Recommended (but not enforced by hounfour) when type=derived_inference. Basis-set validity is consumer-side per ADR-010.

items.description: A sibling claim_id that this inference draws from (DAG edge).

privacy_scope.description: Privacy classification for Assertion payloads. Four substrate-agnostic levels: public | participants_only | controller_only | compliance_only. Vocabulary only — enforcement is consumer-side.

anyOf.description: No privacy constraints; assertion may be shared without restriction.
Assertion content visible only to participants in the deliberation.
Assertion content visible only to the subject's controller_agent_id.
Assertion content visible only to compliance / audit roles; sensitive disclosures.

risk_level.description: Risk classification for Assertion payloads. Four ordinal levels: routine | elevated | high | severe. Vocabulary only — risk thresholds are consumer-side.

anyOf.description: No elevated risk; routine assertion handling.
Elevated risk; consumer policy may require additional review.
High risk; consumer policy typically requires panel review or asymmetric blocker treatment.
Severe risk; consumer policy typically requires immediate halt + sanction review.

recall_scope.description: Environment selector for recall machinery. Closed 5-member core (public | private | system | demo | test) plus a 3-segment consumer namespace fallback. The combined regex (^([a-z]+|[a-z][a-z0-9]+:[a-z][a-z0-9_-]+:[a-z][a-z0-9_-]+)$) matches either form for cross-runner parity.

anyOf.description: Consumer-public surface; recipient may share the recall publicly.
Consumer-private surface; recipient must keep the recall internal.
System / infrastructure surface (logs, telemetry, internal control plane).
Demo / showcase surface; not an audit-grade surface.
Test surface; never an audit-grade surface.
3-segment consumer namespace: <github-org>:<consumer>:<surface>. Hounfour does not register namespaces; uniqueness is consumer-coordination concern.

assertion_class.description: Illocutionary-force vocabulary for Assertion. Substrate-agnostic core of 7 speech-act categories (attestation | observation | assessment | consent | delegation | commitment | disclosure) plus a 3-segment consumer namespace fallback. Member set locked at PR-A2.1 design-freeze gate per D-006.

anyOf.description: Generic "I sign this fact"; the most common form, used when the assertion is a witnessed claim.
"I witnessed X" without a normative judgment; lower-rigor sibling of attestation.
"I evaluate Z" (rating / score / quality); bridges with the reputation domain.
"I authorize Y" (permission grant); paired with delegation but distinct from authority transfer.
"I transfer authority to" (proxy / agency); composes with v8.4.0 OrgRepresentativeDelegation.
"I bind myself to" (promise / pledge); composes with the v8.5.0 CommitmentRoot primitive.
"I disclose facts about" (transparency / report); covers transparency_report / incident_record cases.
3-segment consumer namespace: <github-org>:<consumer>:<class>. Hounfour does not register namespaces; uniqueness is consumer-coordination concern.

confidence.description: Signer-asserted confidence in the assertion content; 0..1 inclusive.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp at which the assertion was created.

contract_version.description: Hounfour contract version this assertion was authored against.

status.description: Pre-admission; no signatures required. The only NON-crypto-bearing variant.

anyOf.description: admitted variant — signatures[] required (A1). Crypto-bearing.

assertion_id.description: Stable opaque identifier for this assertion (UUID v4).

body_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest (lowercase) of safeCanonicalize(assertion-body). NFC + RFC 8785 + 100KB cap per the hashing-spec freeze. The body is the assertion content the signer commits to.

delegation_authority.description: Permissions this agent may delegate. Requires delegation scope >= verified.

max_delegation_depth.description: Maximum delegation chain depth this agent may create.

Assertion content visible only to participants in the deliberation.
Assertion content visible only to the subject's controller_agent_id.
Assertion content visible only to compliance / audit roles; sensitive disclosures.

Elevated risk; consumer policy may require additional review.
High risk; consumer policy typically requires panel review or asymmetric blocker treatment.
Severe risk; consumer policy typically requires immediate halt + sanction review.

The combined regex (^([a-z]+|[a-z][a-z0-9]+:[a-z][a-z0-9_-]+:[a-z][a-z0-9_-]+)$) matches either form for cross-runner parity.

Consumer-private surface; recipient must keep the recall internal.

assertion_class.description: Illocutionary-force vocabulary for Assertion. Substrate-agnostic core of 7 speech-act categories (attestation | observation | assessment | consent | delegation | commitment | disclosure) plus a 3-segment consumer namespace fallback. Member set locked at PR-A2.1 design-freeze gate per D-006.

anyOf.description: Generic "I sign this fact"; the most common form, used when the assertion is a witnessed claim.
"I witnessed X" without a normative judgment; lower-rigor sibling of attestation.
"I evaluate Z" (rating / score / quality); bridges with the reputation domain.
"I authorize Y" (permission grant); paired with delegation but distinct from authority transfer.
"I transfer authority to" (proxy / agency); composes with v8.4.0 OrgRepresentativeDelegation.
"I bind myself to" (promise / pledge); composes with the v8.5.0 CommitmentRoot primitive.

3-segment consumer namespace: <github-org>:<consumer>:<class>. Hounfour does not register namespaces; uniqueness is consumer-coordination concern.

confidence.description: Signer-asserted confidence in the assertion content; 0..1 inclusive.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp at which the assertion was created.

contract_version.description: Hounfour contract version this assertion was authored against.

signatures.description: At least one SignatureEnvelope. Each envelope carries the signed_payload_hash committing to body_hash + the rest of the assertion fields (consumer-side reconstruction).

items.description: Crypto-bearing envelope wrapping a signed payload hash. validate() defaults to { valid: false, errors: [{ code: CRYPTO_DEFERRED }] } unless { acceptDeferred: true } is passed; that opt-in is the safety mechanism preventing "shape valid means trusted" by accident.

envelope_id.description: Stable opaque identifier for this envelope instance (UUID v4).

signature_type.description: Semantic kind of a signature: what the signer is asserting by signing. Orthogonal to the crypto algorithm.

anyOf.description: Signer is asserting a fact they observed or vouch for.
Signer is granting permission for a downstream action.
Signer is binding themselves to a future action or state.
Dev-only marker. NOT a cryptographic guarantee — consumers MUST reject in production.

key_ref.description: Consumer-side reference to the SignerEntry.key_ref that produced this signature. Hounfour does not dereference; the consumer resolves it against its own Keyring.

signed_payload_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest (lowercase) of safeCanonicalize(payload) where payload is the wire artifact being signed minus its own signatures[] and minus any field literally named signed_payload_hash. NFC + RFC 8785 + 100KB cap per the hashing-spec freeze.

signature_value.description: Ed25519 signature value, unpadded base64url-encoded (RFC 4648 §5), prefixed with "ed25519:". A 64-byte Ed25519 signature encodes to exactly 86 unpadded characters; padded forms with "==" are NOT accepted (the character class excludes padding) — producers MUST emit unpadded base64url. Hounfour does NOT verify the signature; consumer-side verification is required (see CRYPTO_DEFERRED manifest entry).

signed_at.description: When the signer produced this signature.

contract_version.description: Hounfour contract version this envelope instance was authored against.

status.description: Signed and accepted. Crypto-bearing — signatures[] required (A1).

anyOf.description: superseded variant — original signatures preserved. Crypto-bearing.

assertion_id.description: Stable opaque identifier for this assertion (UUID v4).

body_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest (lowercase) of safeCanonicalize(assertion-body). NFC + RFC 8785 + 100KB cap per the hashing-spec freeze. The body is the assertion content the signer commits to.

provenance.description: Per-grounding provenance records describing how the assertion content was sourced. ClaimGrounding is EXTENDED in PR-A2.3 with 1-2 substrate-agnostic discriminator members.

items.description: Provenance for a single Claim. type.description: Grounding category determining which auxiliary fields apply.

claim_id.description: Parent claim reference for DAG closure (used by is_valid_dag).

output_hash.description: Canonical sha256:<64-hex> hash; required when type=tool_output.

trust_scopes.description: Per-capability-scope trust levels with default fallback (v6.0.0; v8.5.0 EXTEND adds optional match_strategy + precedence_score per D-007 / OQ-S6).

scopes.description: Per-scope trust levels. Missing scopes fall back to default_level.

envelope_id.description: Stable opaque identifier for this envelope instance (UUID v4).

signature_type.description: Semantic kind of a signature: what the signer is asserting by signing. Orthogonal to the crypto algorithm.

anyOf.description: Signer is asserting a fact they observed or vouch for.
Signer is granting permission for a downstream action.
Signer is binding themselves to a future action or state.
Dev-only marker. NOT a cryptographic guarantee — consumers MUST reject in production.

key_ref.description: Consumer-side reference to the SignerEntry.key_ref that produced this signature. Hounfour does not dereference; the consumer resolves it against its own Keyring.

signed_payload_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest (lowercase) of safeCanonicalize(payload) where payload is the wire artifact being signed minus its own signatures[] and minus any field literally named signed_payload_hash. NFC + RFC 8785 + 100KB cap per the hashing-spec freeze.

signature_value.description: Ed25519 signature value, unpadded base64url-encoded (RFC 4648 §5), prefixed with "ed25519:". A 64-byte Ed25519 signature encodes to exactly 86 unpadded characters; padded forms with "==" are NOT accepted (the character class excludes padding) — producers MUST emit unpadded base64url. Hounfour does NOT verify the signature; consumer-side verification is required (see CRYPTO_DEFERRED manifest entry).

signed_at.description: When the signer produced this signature.

contract_version.description: Hounfour contract version this envelope instance was authored against.

status.description: Under formal challenge; signatures still meaningful for audit chain.

anyOf.description: revoked variant — signatures preserved. Crypto-bearing.

assertion_id.description: Stable opaque identifier for this assertion (UUID v4).

body_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest (lowercase) of safeCanonicalize(assertion-body). NFC + RFC 8785 + 100KB cap per the hashing-spec freeze. The body is the assertion content the signer commits to.

provenance.description: Per-grounding provenance records describing how the assertion content was sourced. ClaimGrounding is EXTENDED in PR-A2.3 with 1-2 substrate-agnostic discriminator members.

items.description: Provenance for a single Claim. Type-specific field requirements enforced by constraint file. v8.5.0 EXTEND: two new substrate-agnostic discriminator members (external_reference, derived_inference) with corresponding optional fields (external_uri, inference_basis); strict-additive on the v8.4.0 surface.

type.description: Grounding category determining which auxiliary fields apply.

artifact_id.description: Parent artifact reference for DAG closure (used by is_valid_dag).

claim_id.description: Parent claim reference for DAG closure (used by is_valid_dag).

output_hash.description: Canonical sha256:<64-hex> hash; required when type=tool_output.

source.description: Canonical agent identity with capability-scoped trust (v6.0.0, BREAKING).

agent_type.description: Classification of agent type in the protocol.

trust_scopes.description: Per-capability-scope trust levels with default fallback (v6.0.0; v8.5.0 EXTEND adds optional match_strategy + precedence_score per D-007 / OQ-S6).

scopes.description: Per-scope trust levels. Missing scopes fall back to default_level.

billing.description: Trust level assigned to an agent. Forms a total order from untrusted to sovereign.

governance.description: Trust level assigned to an agent. Forms a total order from untrusted to sovereign.

inference.description: Trust level assigned to an agent. Forms a total order from untrusted to sovereign.

delegation.description: Trust level assigned to an agent. Forms a total order from untrusted to sovereign.

audit.description: Trust level assigned to an agent. Forms a total order from untrusted to sovereign.

composition.description: Trust level assigned to an agent. Forms a total order from untrusted to sovereign.

default_level.description: Trust level assigned to an agent. Forms a total order from untrusted to sovereign.

match_strategy.description: How a SignerCompetenceRule.required_capability_scopes set is compared against this profile's scopes. Default behavior on absence is "subset" (the trust profile must include every required scope) — preserves v8.4.0 implicit semantics.

precedence_score.description: Tiebreaker when multiple SignerCompetenceRules match the same action. Higher score wins. Absence means "no preference" — consumers fall back to rule-list order.

delegation_authority.description: Permissions this agent may delegate. Requires delegation scope >= verified.

max_delegation_depth.description: Maximum delegation chain depth this agent may create.

contract_version.description: Protocol contract version.

external_uri.description: Off-protocol reference: URL / DOI / chain-transaction id / etc. Substrate-agnostic; consumer parses the URI grammar. Recommended (but not enforced by hounfour) when type=external_reference.

inference_basis.description: Sibling claim_ids the derived inference draws from (DAG edges). Recommended (but not enforced by hounfour) when type=derived_inference. Basis-set validity is consumer-side per ADR-010.

items.description: A sibling claim_id that this inference draws from (DAG edge).

privacy_scope.description: Privacy classification for Assertion payloads. Four substrate-agnostic levels: public | participants_only | controller_only | compliance_only. Vocabulary only — enforcement is consumer-side.

anyOf.description: No privacy constraints; assertion may be shared without restriction.
Assertion content visible only to participants in the deliberation.
Assertion content visible only to the subject's controller_agent_id.
Assertion content visible only to compliance / audit roles; sensitive disclosures.

risk_level.description: Risk classification for Assertion payloads. Four ordinal levels: routine | elevated | high | severe. Vocabulary only — risk thresholds are consumer-side.

anyOf.description: No elevated risk; routine assertion handling.
Elevated risk; consumer policy may require additional review.
High risk; consumer policy typically requires panel review or asymmetric blocker treatment.
Severe risk; consumer policy typically requires immediate halt + sanction review.

recall_scope.description: Environment selector for recall machinery. Closed 5-member core (public | private | system | demo | test) plus a 3-segment consumer namespace fallback. The combined regex (^([a-z]+|[a-z][a-z0-9]+:[a-z][a-z0-9_-]+:[a-z][a-z0-9_-]+)$) matches either form for cross-runner parity.

anyOf.description: Consumer-public surface; recipient may share the recall publicly.
Consumer-private surface; recipient must keep the recall internal.
System / infrastructure surface (logs, telemetry, internal control plane).
Demo / showcase surface; not an audit-grade surface.
Test surface; never an audit-grade surface.
3-segment consumer namespace: <github-org>:<consumer>:<surface>. Hounfour does not register namespaces; uniqueness is consumer-coordination concern.

assertion_class.description: Illocutionary-force vocabulary for Assertion. Substrate-agnostic core of 7 speech-act categories (attestation | observation | assessment | consent | delegation | commitment | disclosure) plus a 3-segment consumer namespace fallback. Member set locked at PR-A2.1 design-freeze gate per D-006.

anyOf.description: Generic "I sign this fact"; the most common form, used when the assertion is a witnessed claim.
"I witnessed X" without a normative judgment; lower-rigor sibling of attestation.
"I evaluate Z" (rating / score / quality); bridges with the reputation domain.
"I authorize Y" (permission grant); paired with delegation but distinct from authority transfer.
"I transfer authority to" (proxy / agency); composes with v8.4.0 OrgRepresentativeDelegation.
"I bind myself to" (promise / pledge); composes with the v8.5.0 CommitmentRoot primitive.
"I disclose facts about" (transparency / report); covers transparency_report / incident_record cases.
3-segment consumer namespace: <github-org>:<consumer>:<class>. Hounfour does not register namespaces; uniqueness is consumer-coordination concern.

confidence.description: Signer-asserted confidence in the assertion content; 0..1 inclusive.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp at which the assertion was created.

contract_version.description: Hounfour contract version this assertion was authored against.

signatures.description: At least one SignatureEnvelope. Each envelope carries the signed_payload_hash committing to body_hash + the rest of the assertion fields (consumer-side reconstruction).

items.description: Crypto-bearing envelope wrapping a signed payload hash. validate() defaults to { valid: false, errors: [{ code: CRYPTO_DEFERRED }] } unless { acceptDeferred: true } is passed; that opt-in is the safety mechanism preventing "shape valid means trusted" by accident.

envelope_id.description: Stable opaque identifier for this envelope instance (UUID v4).

signature_type.description: Semantic kind of a signature: what the signer is asserting by signing. Orthogonal to the crypto algorithm.

anyOf.description: Signer is asserting a fact they observed or vouch for.
Signer is granting permission for a downstream action.
Signer is binding themselves to a future action or state.
Dev-only marker. NOT a cryptographic guarantee — consumers MUST reject in production.

key_ref.description: Consumer-side reference to the SignerEntry.key_ref that produced this signature. Hounfour does not dereference; the consumer resolves it against its own Keyring.

signed_payload_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest (lowercase) of safeCanonicalize(payload) where payload is the wire artifact being signed minus its own signatures[] and minus any field literally named signed_payload_hash. NFC + RFC 8785 + 100KB cap per the hashing-spec freeze.

signature_value.description: Ed25519 signature value, unpadded base64url-encoded (RFC 4648 §5), prefixed with "ed25519:". A 64-byte Ed25519 signature encodes to exactly 86 unpadded characters; padded forms with "==" are NOT accepted (the character class excludes padding) — producers MUST emit unpadded base64url. Hounfour does NOT verify the signature; consumer-side verification is required (see CRYPTO_DEFERRED manifest entry).

signed_at.description: When the signer produced this signature.

contract_version.description: Hounfour contract version this envelope instance was authored against.

status.description: Invalidated by sanction; original signatures preserved for audit chain.

anyOf.description: forgotten variant — signatures preserved when forget_scope ∈ {pii_only, agent_full, pii_and_link_to_key}; absent only when forget_scope == crypto_full_destruction (consumer-side state). Crypto-bearing.

assertion_id.description: Stable opaque identifier for this assertion (UUID v4).

body_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest (lowercase) of safeCanonicalize(assertion-body). NFC + RFC 8785 + 100KB cap per the hashing-spec freeze. The body is the assertion content the signer commits to.

provenance.description: Per-grounding provenance records describing how the assertion content was sourced. ClaimGrounding is EXTENDED in PR-A2.3 with 1-2 substrate-agnostic discriminator members.

Assertion content visible only to the subject's controller_agent_id.
Assertion content visible only to compliance / audit roles; sensitive disclosures.

risk_level.description: Risk classification for Assertion payloads. Four ordinal levels: routine | elevated | high | severe. Vocabulary only — risk thresholds are consumer-side.

anyOf.description: No elevated risk; routine assertion handling.
Elevated risk; consumer policy may require additional review.
High risk; consumer policy typically requires panel review or asymmetric blocker treatment.
Severe risk; consumer policy typically requires immediate halt + sanction review.

recall_scope.description: Environment selector for recall machinery. Closed 5-member core (public | private | system | demo | test) plus a 3-segment consumer namespace fallback. The combined regex (^([a-z]+|[a-z][a-z0-9]+:[a-z][a-z0-9_-]+:[a-z][a-z0-9_-]+)$) matches either form for cross-runner parity.

anyOf.description: Consumer-public surface; recipient may share the recall publicly.
Consumer-private surface; recipient must keep the recall internal.
System / infrastructure surface (logs, telemetry, internal control plane).
Demo / showcase surface; not an audit-grade surface.
Test surface; never an audit-grade surface.
3-segment consumer namespace: <github-org>:<consumer>:<surface>. Hounfour does not register namespaces; uniqueness is consumer-coordination concern.

assertion_class.description: Illocutionary-force vocabulary for Assertion. Substrate-agnostic core of 7 speech-act categories (attestation | observation | assessment | consent | delegation | commitment | disclosure) plus a 3-segment consumer namespace fallback. Member set locked at PR-A2.1 design-freeze gate per D-006.

anyOf.description: Generic "I sign this fact"; the most common form, used when the assertion is a witnessed claim.
"I witnessed X" without a normative judgment; lower-rigor sibling of attestation.
"I evaluate Z" (rating / score / quality); bridges with the reputation domain.
"I authorize Y" (permission grant); paired with delegation but distinct from authority transfer.
"I transfer authority to" (proxy / agency); composes with v8.4.0 OrgRepresentativeDelegation.
"I bind myself to" (promise / pledge); composes with the v8.5.0 CommitmentRoot primitive.
"I disclose facts about" (transparency / report); covers transparency_report / incident_record cases.
3-segment consumer namespace: <github-org>:<consumer>:<class>. Hounfour does not register namespaces; uniqueness is consumer-coordination concern.

confidence.description: Signer-asserted confidence in the assertion content; 0..1 inclusive.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp at which the assertion was created.

contract_version.description: Hounfour contract version this assertion was authored against.

signatures.description: At least one SignatureEnvelope. Each envelope carries the signed_payload_hash committing to body_hash + the rest of the assertion fields (consumer-side reconstruction).

items.description: Crypto-bearing envelope wrapping a signed payload hash. validate() defaults to { valid: false, errors: [{ code: CRYPTO_DEFERRED }] } unless { acceptDeferred: true } is passed; that opt-in is the safety mechanism preventing "shape valid means trusted" by accident.

envelope_id.description: Stable opaque identifier for this envelope instance (UUID v4).

signature_type.description: Semantic kind of a signature: what the signer is asserting by signing. Orthogonal to the crypto algorithm.

anyOf.description: Signer is asserting a fact they observed or vouch for.
Signer is granting permission for a downstream action.
Signer is binding themselves to a future action or state.
Dev-only marker. NOT a cryptographic guarantee — consumers MUST reject in production.

key_ref.description: Consumer-side reference to the SignerEntry.key_ref that produced this signature. Hounfour does not dereference; the consumer resolves it against its own Keyring.

signed_payload_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest (lowercase) of safeCanonicalize(payload) where payload is the wire artifact being signed minus its own signatures[] and minus any field literally named signed_payload_hash. NFC + RFC 8785 + 100KB cap per the hashing-spec freeze.

signature_value.description: Ed25519 signature value, unpadded base64url-encoded (RFC 4648 §5), prefixed with "ed25519:". A 64-byte Ed25519 signature encodes to exactly 86 unpadded characters; padded forms with "==" are NOT accepted (the character class excludes padding) — producers MUST emit unpadded base64url. Hounfour does NOT verify the signature; consumer-side verification is required (see CRYPTO_DEFERRED manifest entry).

signed_at.description: When the signer produced this signature.

contract_version.description: Hounfour contract version this envelope instance was authored against.

status.description: Moved to cold storage; signatures preserved for provenance.
